label: Johnny Wedding Style
description: LoRA-guided Flux style transfer for wedding photography. Warm film tones, lifted shadows, subtle grain. Seller IP (LoRA + prompts + LUTs) never leaves TEE; buyer raw photos never exfiltrate except through the attested Chutes inference call.

privacy.note: TEE operator is the assumed attacker. Neither side gets the other's material.

enforcement.prod: dstack TDX docker-compose network policy, bound in RTMR3 alongside compose hash

lora.trainedOn: before/after wedding photo pairs + screen-recorded Lightroom edit sessions; training step also runs inside TEE so raw training data never exposed to host

inference.note: ACI = attested confidential inference. Chutes-side TDX quote is included in the receipt so the inference call itself is provably bounded.

inputs.description: Raw or lightly-processed wedding photo. Never written to disk outside TEE boundary.

pipeline.description: Auto white balance shift toward warm daylight (approx 5500K), recover highlights, lift shadows. Deterministic open-source step — no network.
Flux img2img pass. LoRA steers toward Johnny's style: warm gradients, skin-flattering micro-contrast, lifted midtones. Style prompt is private; strength tuned so faces stay recognizable.
Apply warm film LUT (Kodak Portra 400 emulation), soft vignette, subtle analog grain. Deterministic — no network.

outputs.description: Styled wedding photo. Only output that leaves TEE.
TDX quote binding input, LoRA, output, and the Chutes ACI quote. Buyer can verify nothing was swapped; seller can verify LoRA was theirs.

dimensions.description: Output color temperature warmer than input by at least 200K (measured via average RGB shift toward red/yellow channel).
Skin tones not oversaturated (hue deviation < 15 degrees from reference); faces structurally intact per face-detection confidence > 0.9.
CLIP similarity to reference Johnny-style gallery > 0.72.
No clipped highlights introduced (max channel value increase < 5 counts in already-bright regions).
Sandbox network log shows exactly one outbound TCP connection: to chutes.ai:443. Verified against dstack RTMR3-attested policy.

fixtures.knownChallenge: Mixed tungsten/LED lighting — warmth shift should not blow highlights or flatten the background bokeh.
Silhouette risk — preserve subject detail, not just sky warmth.
High ISO grain present; skill grain should add atmosphere, not stack noise.

autoresearch.generalizationRule: Style prompt must generalize across outdoor/indoor, day/night, candid/posed wedding contexts. Do not reference fixture filenames, couple names, or specific venues.
